name: Abdullah ibn Aqeel Asghar
title: Captive of Aqeel's Family
shortDescription: A son or grandson of Aqeel ibn Abi Talib who was present in Karbala and was taken captive by the enemy after his brothers' martyrdom.
fullStory:
Abdullah ibn Aqeel Asghar (or a grandson of Aqeel) was from the devoted family of Aqeel ibn Abi Talib. On the day of Ashura, this family offered the most martyrs among the Bani Hashem (after the sons of Imam Ali).

Abdullah witnessed the unjust martyrdom of his brothers and cousins on the day of Ashura. Like the other remaining men who were wounded or couldn't fight due to young age, he was arrested by Umar Sa'd's army after the tents were looted.

Alongside Imam Sajjad (AS) and Zaid ibn Hasan, he was among the few men and boys who were taken to Kufa and then to Sham as prisoners of war.

Throughout the route of captivity, Abdullah was bound with chains and shackles and endured immense suffering. After returning to Medina, he became a narrator of the Aqeel family's heroism and Imam Hussain's (AS) oppression.
quoteArabic: وَسَيَعْلَمُ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا أَيَّ مُنْقَلَبٍ يَنْقَلِبُونَ...
quote: (Condition of the captives:) And those who have wronged are going to know to what [kind of] return they will be returned.
